items.title: [Boing Boing] A Pulitzer winner wrote a short story without using the letter "I"
items.content_html:
In 1969, French author Georges Perec wrote La Disparition, an entire novel that does not contain the letter E. In 1994, Gilbert Adair translated it into English as A Void — also without a single E. I've only read portions of it, and I'm still amazed that someone could tell a coherent story without the most common letter in the English language. — Read the rest

The post A Pulitzer winner wrote a short story without using the letter "I" appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Artist paints Lovecraft's Great Old Ones into all 46 of Hokusai's woodblock prints
items.content_html:
Katsushika Hokusai drew tentacled sea creatures in the 1800s. H.P. Lovecraft built a mythology around them in the 1920s. Japanese illustrator Goki Yamada has now put them together in Thirty-six Views of Evil Gods (邪神三十六景), a 128-page art book that inserts Cthulhu, Nyarlathotep, and other Great Old Ones into every plate of Hokusai's famous ukiyo-e series "Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji" — the woodblock prints that gave us "The Great Wave off Kanagawa." — Read the rest

The post Artist paints Lovecraft's Great Old Ones into all 46 of Hokusai's woodblock prints appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [Boing Boing] A clown fought a robot in 1897, and we can finally watch it
items.content_html:
A 45-second film from 1897 — the earliest known cinematic depiction of a robot-like figure — has been rediscovered after being lost for over a century. A nitrate print of Georges Méliès' Gugusse et l'Automate turned up in Michigan and was restored by the Library of Congress, which means you can now watch it for the first time in generations. — Read the rest

The post A clown fought a robot in 1897, and we can finally watch it appeared first on Boing Boing.
